reforms.description: Chile is enhancing its R&D support by balancing direct support and tax credits to improve overall research and development spending. The tax reform proposal includes stronger incentives for productivity-enhancing investments, particularly in research and development, and modifications to the R&D tax credit, including higher ceilings and simplifications for SMEs. Public support for innovation and technology upgrading is being strengthened to boost productivity, with a focus on better coordination and alignment with strategic priorities such as sustainability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to mobilize additional resources for innovation and enhance productivity through better R&D support mechanisms.
reforms.status_evidence: Public support for innovation and technology upgrading is being strengthened and has been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant overhaul of the R&D tax credit system and public support mechanisms, aiming for a substantial increase in R&D spending.
reforms.source_quote: A draft tax reform submitted to Congress in 2022 includes new modifications to the R&D tax credit, including higher ceilings, simplifications of the application procedure for these benefits...

reforms.description: The Chilean government plans to expand support for research, development, and innovation, aiming to allocate an additional 0.3% of GDP towards these areas to enhance productivity and innovation capacity. This initiative is part of a broader effort to strengthen and better coordinate policies to promote business R&D, aligning them with strategic priorities such as sustainability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public funding for R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to expand support for R&D and has legislated this initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: The allocation of an additional 0.3% of GDP towards R&D represents a significant commitment to enhancing public funding for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Overall spending on research and development (R&D) and innovation is relatively low, and only a minor fraction of this spending comes from the business sector.

reforms.description: A new partial VAT exemption for start-ups in their first year of operation is part of the current tax reform proposal, aimed at supporting entrepreneurship with a low fiscal cost.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The VAT exemption is designed to lower the financial burden on new start-ups, encouraging entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The VAT exemption for start-ups is part of the current tax reform proposal and has been announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the reform is beneficial for start-ups, it is a targeted measure rather than a broad systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: A new partial VAT exemption for start-ups in their first year of operation, part of the current tax reform proposal, is likely to support entrepreneurship at a fairly low fiscal cost.

reforms.description: Chile established a new 'Committee for the Development of the Green Hydrogen Industry' to accelerate the development of the green hydrogen sector, coordinating actions across multiple ministries to address local demand, project financing, and regulatory issues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the committee aims to foster the growth of a strategic sector, enhancing innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The committee has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a dedicated committee for a strategic sector represents a significant policy initiative.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2022, a new 'Committee for the Development of the Green Hydrogen Industry' was established to accelerate the development of this industry.
